items.title: Young stroke survivor works to raise awareness as NT falls behind

children.content: When Alison Reidy, then 38, woke in terrible pain, she had no idea she was having a stroke. Now she's trying to improve stroke awareness in the Northern Territory.

image.altText: A woman looking at the camera with a serious facial expression as she sits in front of gym equipment.

items.title: DV shelter death inquest hears from top police, child safety representatives

children.content: A coronial inquest hears that Northern Territory authorities assessed that a father posed "no risk" to his child, despite allegations he repeatedly abused his partner and shook the baby.

image.altText: Darwin local court interior.

items.title: Horse trainer's mission to spotlight 'amazing' animal companions used in WWI

children.content: Every year on Anzac Day, for more than two decades, Janette Wilson saddles up her Waler horse and leads the procession for the Darwin march, representing the light horsemen and their companions who served in World War I.

articles.title: 101yo Bombing of Darwin veteran wants Australians to know what unfolded

children.content: At 101 years old, Bombing of Darwin survivor Terrance Byrne wants Australians to know exactly what unfolded during the World War II attack on Darwin — still the single largest foreign attack on Australian soil.

image.altText: An older man sitting in front of table of framed black and white pictures.

gallery.altText: A woman, smiling widely, standing next to a horse with a red poppy tucked behind its ear, in a horse pen.
A Waler horse standing in a pen, with lots of shade and leafy green trees.
A black and white photo of uniformed soldiers on horses, lined up in front of pyramids in Egypt.

thumbnail.altText: A woman, smiling widely, standing next to a horse with a red poppy tucked behind its ear, in a horse pen.
A Waler horse standing in a pen, with lots of shade and leafy green trees.
A black and white photo of uniformed soldiers on horses, lined up in front of pyramids in Egypt.

items.title: Boy, 16, killed, two others injured in Wadeye quad bike crash

children.content: A 16-year-old boy has been killed in a quad bike crash that also injured a 12-year-old boy and a 19-year-old man in Wadeye on Wednesday.

image.altText: Close up image of an NT Police sign, behind barbed wire fence, with left of frame slightly obstructed by palm tree leaves.

items.title: 'How many beers have you had?' Flood evacuees claim racial profiling

children.content: Flood evacuees from remote Northern Territory communities say they are being "treated like prisoners" by security guards working at their emergency accommodation centre.

image.altText: Signs attached to black fencing reads: no alcohol, no visitors

items.title: Police 'not fully prepared' for violent scene they were called to, NT inquest hears

children.content: An inquest hears evidence from a senior police officer who ran into a room to disarm a mother who was self-harming inside a Darwin crisis accommodation facility, describing the incident as "one of the worst days" of her policing career.

image.altText: A police car outside the Darwin Local Court.

items.title: New $30.5m funding package to help repair NT roads damaged by flooding

children.content: The Northern Territory government has announced $30.5 million in funding to repair roads damaged by wet season flooding across the territory.

image.altText: A remote road collapsed to one side.

items.title: Kingpin's private phone calls break open Mongols' 'culture of silence'

children.content: Detectives say bikies are recruiting inside Northern Territory prisons, as a kingpin's tapped private phone calls offer a rare glimpse into the underground world of outlaw motorcycle gangs.

gallery.altText: A red white and blue skull embroidered into patches sewn into vests.

thumbnail.altText: A red white and blue skull embroidered into patches sewn into vests.

items.title: End to 'let it rip' NDIS provider spending and claims submitted 'on serviettes'

children.content: NDIS Minister Mark Butler will end the practice of handwritten invoices, providers without qualifications and a "let it rip" market of profiteering operators. But the sector warns changes must be navigated carefully.

articles.title: The NDIS gave participants their lives back. Now they fear what's to come

children.content: NDIS participants set up their lives around the scheme's rules. But now the rules are changing and they fear what lies ahead.

image.altText: A white gender-diverse young adult with long red hair sitting in a wheelchair at a kitchen table

articles.title: NDIS cuts a 'slap in the face, punch in the guts' for those needing care in regional Australia

children.content: People living with disability in regional areas already face extra hurdles in accessing support and families fear changes to the NDIS could have irreversible consequences.

image.altText: A young boy and girl both wearing glasses

gallery.altText: An NDIS logo is seen on a building

thumbnail.altText: An NDIS logo is seen on a building

items.title: 'Genuinely speechless': Appeal board's stunning claim in homophobia case

children.content: The appeal board in the Lance Collard homophobia case says homophobic and racist language is commonplace in the AFL due to the "highly competitive" nature of the game.

image.altText: Lance Collard stands with both arms in the air during a St Kilda vs West Coast Eagles AFL match in 2025.

items.title: 'They deserve death': Officer used dispatch system to monitor police wife

children.content: Text messages read in court set out how a Tasmania Police officer monitored his wife's movements, including via an internal dispatching system, as part of a 15-month family violence matter.

image.altText: A man seen in side profile view.

items.title: Mapping the most critical shipping lanes in the world

children.content: Oil prices have spiked since the Strait of Hormuz was closed. With about 90 per cent of all traded goods shipped by sea, what are the other key shipping lanes in the world?

image.altText: A map of different international waterways across the world.

items.title: Australia is searching for productivity. Could reducing inequality be the answer?

children.content: Is reducing inequality and increasing social mobility one of Australia's best paths to improving our lacklustre economic productivity performance?

image.altText: Unidentifiable kids in a classroom.

items.title: ASX 200 down after Wall Street losses, oil prices spike again

children.content: Oil prices are spiking again on global markets, and the ASX is trading in negative territory on the final day of the week. It comes after the Nasdaq shed almost 1 per cent. Follow the day's events and insights as they happen with our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: Pentagon awards $276m AUKUS contract funded by Australia

children.content: The US Department of Defense announces the first major submarine contract to deliver on the AUKUS security deal.

image.altText: A crowd of people standing on a submarine.

items.title: Tradwives, gangsters and one very special dog: April's 10 best books

children.content: This month's top picks include new works from Steve Toltz and Colm Tóibín, a satire inspired by Yellowface and an investigation into the death of a London teen who masqueraded as the son of a Russian oligarch.

articles.title: New Writers' Week boss says books, not political views, will inform line-up

children.content: The new chair of Adelaide Writers' Week has sent a statement to would-be critics, quipping that Pauline Hanson would be worthy of inclusion on a literary line-up if the One Nation leader were "secretly writing Australia's next best crime novel".

image.altText: A smiling woman leans an arm over a park bench

gallery.altText: Ten book covers on a blue background.

thumbnail.altText: Ten book covers on a blue background.

items.title: Jane Fonda was in her 40s when she changed the way we exercise

children.content: In the 1980s, the fitness industry as we know it was just getting started. One of the most important people in developing the way we exercise today was Jane Fonda, who released a best-selling aerobics video in 1982.

image.altText: Actress Jane Fonda in a yellow exercise outfit stretching with her left foot pointed upward in the air.

items.title: Weekly news quiz: Trump's eleventh-hour announcement, a disabled bird and a speedy humanoid robot

children.content: It's Friday, and that means it's time to test your news knowledge from the past seven days. Were you paying attention this week?

image.altText: A red and black robot on a street with barriers in the background and a ribbon falling to the ground.

items.title: Hundreds of fishing boats sit idle off southern China — but they're not there to fish

children.content: Off China's southern coast, hundreds of fishing boats sit idle. The crews aren't there to fish, but rather to "maintain presence" as part of a shadowy, state-funded fleet.

gallery.altText: A satellite image giving a birdseye view of boats scattered across a bay.

thumbnail.altText: A satellite image giving a birdseye view of boats scattered across a bay.

items.title: Police dismiss claims organised crime linked to farmer's murder

children.content: Fake images and false claims about Richard Wills's death are circulating on social media, prompting police to speak out about the investigation into the 65-year-old farmer's murder.

image.altText: Man wearing black cap holding a baby

items.title: Teen killer of Qld grandmother loses sentence appeal

children.content: The 16-year sentence handed to the teenager who murdered south east Queensland woman Vyleen White has been upheld by the state's Court of Appeal.

image.altText: a brunette woman wearing a white blazer

items.title: Dozen more possible human burial sites found at Rottnest Island church

children.content: Authorities believe there may be a dozen more unmarked Aboriginal burial sites near where human remains were found on Western Australia's tourist haven Rottnest Island earlier this month.

image.altText: A look over rock groynes at boats in a bay at Rottnest with a lighthouse in the background.

items.title: Perth woman warns about pedicure safety after severe infection

children.content: Kyla Willcox says she almost lost her toe from a potentially fatal infection after having a pedicure at a Perth nail salon.

image.altText: Foot with big toe covered in white bandage

items.title: 'Nice to be nice': Duo help collapsed runner finish Boston Marathon

children.content: Aaron Beggs, who helped fellow runner Ajay Haridasse across the Boston Marathon finish line, says "the natural instinct was just to go and pick him up".

gallery.altText: Aaron Beggs and Robson de Oliveria help Ajay Haridasse

thumbnail.altText: Aaron Beggs and Robson de Oliveria help Ajay Haridasse

items.title: 'This is now terminal': UK braces for prospect of sixth PM in seven years

children.content: Sir Keir Starmer, who led Labour to a landslide general election victory in July 2024, has become so unpopular that even some colleagues are framing his demise as a case of not if, but when.

image.altText: A man in a suit looking down

items.title: Prince Harry urges Putin to 'stop this war' on visit to Ukraine

children.content: Prince Harry makes a surprise visit to Kyiv, urging Russia's President Vladimir Putin to end the war and calling on US President Donald Trump to show leadership to help resolve the conflict in Ukraine.

image.altText: Britain's Prince Harry speaks at the Kyiv Security Forum, he is stood at a lectern behind a microphone

items.title: Tobacco ban in the UK could create smoke-free generation

children.content: Anyone born after 2008 will be banned from buying cigarettes or vapes for life in the UK when a new bill becomes law.

image.altText: An unidentifiable person's hands holding an unlit cigarette and a lit red lighter.

items.title: US soldier charged with making $560,000 on Maduro removal bets

children.content: In the weeks leading up to Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro's January 3 capture, Gannon Ken Van Dyke used sensitive classified information to make wagers on prediction market Polymarket.

image.altText: Nicolas Maduro being perp walked by multiple heavily armed police.

items.title: 'Finfluencers' suspected of giving unlawful advice hit with warning notices

children.content: The warning notice to the so-called "finfluencers" relates to suspected provision of unlicensed financial advice, including promoting claims of guaranteed returns.

image.altText: hands holding a phone as the stock market runs behind it

items.title: Italy dismisses 'shameful' idea to replace Iran at football World Cup

children.content: Italian officials say they are not interested in replacing Iran at the FIFA World Cup, after the idea was raised by a Trump ally.

image.altText: A man in a bright blue suit walks down the street.

items.title: Whale rescuers describe skill and courage needed to detangle 40-tonne marine mammal

children.content: As thousands of whales begin their annual northern migration up the east coast of Australia, teams of specialist rescuers are preparing to help them along the way.

gallery.altText: Aerial view of two boats on the water reaching towards a fake whale tail.

thumbnail.altText: Aerial view of two boats on the water reaching towards a fake whale tail.

items.title: Living on idyllic headland poses health problems for mob of kangaroos

children.content: Kangaroos lying on a NSW mid north coast headland may be a "selfie" dream for tourists, but some are becoming weak from malnutrition.

image.altText: three kangaroos on a beach in dune grass

items.title: Ancient fossils reveal when the iconic Twelve Apostles formed

children.content: An analysis of tiny fossils of sea creatures found in rock samples around the Port Campbell region in south-western Victoria has refined our understanding of how the Twelve Apostles formed.

image.altText: Four limestone monoliths of varying size lapped at by shoreline waves next to massive cliffs.

items.title: Patriots NFL coach to sit out draft after photo publication

children.content: The New England Patriots say they support head coach Mike Vrabel's decision to miss the final day of the NFL Draft to seek counselling following publication of photos with journalist Dianna Russini.

image.altText: Mike Vrabel looks to one side

items.title: Figure skating teen makes history for Australia

children.content: At just 15, Hana Bath became the first Australian female to ever land a triple axel in competition. The rising star now has her eye on the 2030 Winter Olympics.

image.altText: A figure skater wearing skates on ice holders her hands in the air
